name: The Earth's Heart
description: A recent rockslide has exposed a cluster of massive, crystalline geodes embedded in the cliffside. They shimmer with an internal, multifaceted light.

geode-initial.text: The stones are beautiful, but their shells are incredibly hard. They seem to contain rare gems within.

choices.label: [Dwarf] Use your knowledge of stone to find the weak points.
[Barbarian/Fighter] Sunder the geodes with raw power.
Use a Hammer to carefully crack one open.
Admire the beauty and continue on.

dwarf-stonework-success.text: With a single, expertly placed tap, the geode shatters along its natural fault lines, revealing a bounty of perfect gems.

sunder-check.success_text: A mighty blow shatters the outer shell. You manage to recover several large gemstone fragments from the debris.
sunder-check.failure_text: The stone is harder than it looks. Your weapon bounces off, sending a painful jar through your arms.

sunder-fail.text: The impact leaves your hands numb. (Took 2 Bludgeoning Damage)

hammer-success.text: It takes time and patience, but you manage to chip away enough of the shell to retrieve a few modest crystals.

exit.text: The crystals catch the sun's light as you walk away.
